free_text: Angular distributions and cross-section ratios for
the reactions 2H(d,n)3He and 2H(d,p)3H below 500 keV
Deuterium gas of purity greater than 99.5% at
0.6 torr pressure
The charged particle detectors were
well-collimated, 5 mm diameter, surface barrier
detectors, of 3000 omega*cm resistivity, n-type silicon
When reverse biased at 30V D.C., these detectors have
a depletion depth of 150 um, which is equal to the
range of 4.1 MeV protons in silicon. Thus, the charged
particles being observed, namely 1H, 3H, 3He, were all
totally stopped in the detectors.
No information about source of uncertainty
Compilation produced by Arzamas RFNC-VNIIEF
CNPD: corrected 4-dig. year and lower cases.
SD: Corrections in all Subents.
No explanation of relative units is given.
